steps.title: Identify the student's goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the distance covered by the Philadelphia and Lancaster Turnpike. The notes list four distinct facts: it was built between 1792 and 1794, it was the first private turnpike in the US, it connected Philadelphia and Lancaster in Pennsylvania, and it was sixty-two miles long. Only the sixty-two-mile measurement directly conveys distance.

steps.title: Test each choice for whether it emphasizes distance
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The sixty-two-mile-long Philadelphia and Lancaster Turnpike connected the Pennsylvania cities of Philadelphia and Lancaster.': Makes "sixty-two-mile-long" a compound adjective that is the defining descriptor of the turnpike, placing distance front and center. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The Philadelphia and Lancaster Turnpike was the first private turnpike in the United States.': Emphasizes the turnpike's historical significance as the first private turnpike. Distance is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The Philadelphia and Lancaster Turnpike, which connected two Pennsylvania cities, was built between 1792 and 1794.': Emphasizes the construction dates. Distance is omitted. ✗
  • Choice D — 'A historic Pennsylvania road, the Philadelphia and Lancaster Turnpike was completed in 1794.': Emphasizes the completion year and historic status. Distance is not mentioned. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
